D4 Colorimetry L3 Apply Reference

Standard Illuminant Spectra

toggle A, D50, D65, or FL11 and watch a smooth daylight curve give way to a fluorescent's jagged spikes

An illuminant is defined by how much power it emits at each wavelength. That spectral shape — not just its colour temperature — decides how objects under it appear.

Toggle each CIE illuminant. Incandescent A climbs steadily into the red; daylight D50 and D65 sweep smoothly; fluorescent FL11 erupts in narrow mercury spikes that make metamerism notoriously hard to manage.
